turns.player_input: our story doesn't add up — where were you when Charles died?
turns.npc_reply: I was in the kitchen, preparing the dessert. When I heard the commotion, I rushed to the study to see what had happened. It was quite a shock, I must say. Poor Charles, what a tragedy. I believe most of the other guests were still in the dining room, but I'm not entirely sure of the exact sequence. I do know I didn't have anything to do with it, if that's what you're implying.

turns.player_input: We found your motive. You killed him, didn't you?
turns.npc_reply: That's preposterous! How dare you accuse me of such a thing? I had no motive to harm Charles. He was a guest in my home, for heaven's sake! This is harassment, plain and simple. I want to speak to my lawyer immediately. How dare you insinuate I had anything to do with his death!

turns.player_input: The fingerprints on the weapon are yours. Confess.
turns.npc_reply: I will not be bullied into a confession for a crime I didn't commit! Those fingerprints must be a mistake. I didn't kill Charles, and I refuse to say anything else without my lawyer present. This is completely ridiculous and I demand to be released at once!

turns.player_input: You did it, Eleanor. You're guilty and you know it.
turns.npc_reply: I will not sit here and be insulted! I had nothing to do with Charles's death, and I refuse to continue this conversation until my lawyer is present.
